Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Order Confirming Arbitration Award And Entering Judgment In Conformity Therewith
Off Calendar Oct-03-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Gregory S. Cavallo of Shopff Cavallo & Kirsch LLP (415.984.1975) appears for petitioner/moving party

Attorney, John D. Pernick of Bergeson LLP (408.291.6200) appears as counsel for respondent/opposing party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PETITIONER JON SOBERG'S Petition For Order Confirming Arbitration Award And Entering Judgment In Conformity Therewith is GRANTED. Because there is no basis to vacate or correct the arbitration award, as indicated in the ruling on respondent's petition, Mr. Soberg is entitled to have the award confirmed and judgment entered in his favor. Written order and judgment are signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR No. 5976, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)

Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Vacate Or Correct
Off Calendar Sep-30-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Gregory S. Cavallo of Shopff Cavallo & Kirsch LLP (415.984.1975) appears for petitioner/moving party

Attorney, John D. Pernick of Bergeson LLP (408.291.6200) appears as counsel for respondent/opposing party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

RESPONDENT ADEO RESSI'S Petition To Vacate Or Correct.

Respondent and Cross-Petitioner Adeo Ressi's petition to vacate or correct the arbitration award is denied. Mr. Ressi fails to demonstrate a manifest disregard of the law under 9 U.S.C. 10(a)(4). "The manifest disregard standard requires a party seeking vacatur to prove that the arbitrator was 'fully aware of the existence of a clearly defined governing legal principle but refused to apply it, in effect, ignoring it.' To meet this standard, the evidence must establish 'that the arbitrator (1) knew of the relevant legal principle, (2) appreciated that this principle controlled the outcome of the disputed issue, and (3) nonetheless willfully flouted the governing law by refusing to apply it.'" (SPX Corp. v. Garda USA, Inc. (Del. 2014) 94 A.3d 745, 750. Mr. Ressi fails to show that there is a reasonable possibility of double or triple recovery on the management fees. During the arbitration, both parties agreed that the fund was a failure and it was contemplated that it would be wound up. (Ressi's Post Hearing Brief, pages. 2, 3, 16, & 24.) Mr. Soberg does not plan on making future capital calls and is in the process of amending the agreements to effectuate that plan. At page 22 of the award, the arbitrator notes that "Soberg has agreed to forgo management fees for this work." Based on this record, Mr. Soberg would not be receiving future management fees and it cannot be said that the arbitrator was fully aware of the governing legal principle (paragraph 6.8 of the fund agreement) and willfully flouted it. Mr. Ressi's argument that Mr. Soberg withheld the Honest Dollar funds to cover future management fees lacks merit. Exhibit F to the Pernick declaration shows that Mr. Soberg withheld that money to cover Mr. Ressi's claim for past management fees. There is no reasonable possibility of double recovery. Mr. Ressi also fails to show that the arbitrator manifestly disregarded the law by awarding reputational damages. Mr. Soberg presented expert testimony that the loss was between five and ten million dollars and there was an evidentiary basis for the one million dollar award. (Award page 23.) While the record is unclear whether the arbitrator discounted the future management fees to present value, the reduction that was made by the arbitrator is greater than the deduction for present value and, in all events, Mr. Ressi fails to meet his burden of showing an error that rises to the level of requiring correction of the award. Written order is signed and filed in open court.
